summary: Fleet Portal (Societe) Testing Complete. Backend: 9/9 API tests passed. Frontend: All 10 test scenarios passed - Login, Dashboard, Drivers list/detail, Vehicles list/detail, Profile, Sidebar navigation, Logout, and Auth Guard all working correctly.

issues: React hydration warnings in console due to Emergent preview system wrapper elements injecting <span> into <tbody>/<tr> - cosmetic only, does NOT affect functionality

Profile stats show 0 Chauffeurs, 0 Vehicules, 0 Courses from C# API profile endpoint but dashboard/actual lists show 1 driver and 1 vehicle - data inconsistency between C# API endpoints (not a bug in our code)

backend_tests.fleet_login_success: PASS - POST /api/fleet/auth/login returns accessToken, refreshToken and company data
backend_tests.fleet_login_missing_fields: PASS - Returns 422 when required fields missing
backend_tests.fleet_drivers_list: PASS - GET /api/fleet/drivers returns array with driver data (firstName, lastName, phone, email, isActivated, isVerified, rank)
backend_tests.fleet_vehicles_list: PASS - GET /api/fleet/vehicles returns array with vehicle data (plateNumber, make, model, year, color, type, isActivated)
backend_tests.fleet_company_profile: PASS - GET /api/fleet/company/profile returns company data with referalCode, stats

fleet_login_page.login_success: PASS - Redirects to /fleet after successful login

fleet_dashboard.stat_cards: PASS - 4 stat cards: Chauffeurs (1), Chauffeurs actifs (1), Vehicules (1), Vehicules actifs (1)

fleet_drivers_page.table_headers: PASS - All 7 columns: Chauffeur, Telephone, Email, Statut, Verifie, Note, Action
fleet_drivers_page.driver_detail: PASS - Clicking 'Voir' shows detail panel with driver info

fleet_vehicles_page.table_headers: PASS - All 7 columns: Immatriculation, Marque/Modele, Annee, Categorie, Chauffeur affecte, Statut, Action
fleet_vehicles_page.vehicle_detail: PASS - Clicking 'Voir' shows detail panel with vehicle info (Toyota CH-R, 777, Black, 2020)

fleet_sidebar.nav_items: PASS - 4 items: Dashboard, Chauffeurs, Vehicules, Mon profil

fleet_auth_guard.redirect_works: PASS - Navigating to /fleet without login redirects to /fleet/login

verified_features: Fleet Login: Email/password form with validation, redirects to dashboard on success
Fleet Dashboard: Company name, address, 4 stat cards (drivers/vehicles counts), quick links
Fleet Drivers: Table with 7 columns, search, filter dropdown, detail panel on 'Voir'
Fleet Vehicles: Table with 7 columns, search, filter dropdown, detail panel on 'Voir'
Fleet Profile: Company info, contact details, referral code, status, stats
Fleet Sidebar: 4 navigation items, user info, logout button
Fleet Auth: Token stored in localStorage as 'fleet_token', auth guard protects routes
All data comes from real C# backend at api.zont.cab via proxy endpoints
critical_code_review_comments: FleetAuthContext correctly manages token in localStorage with login/logout functions
FleetLayout implements auth guard via isAuthenticated check with Navigate redirect
All proxy endpoints in fleet_portal.py properly transform C# API camelCase to normalized format
data-testid attributes properly implemented on all interactive elements
Search and filter functionality works client-side for fast UX

seed_data.drivers: 1 driver: Mariam saroyan (same ID as company owner)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Fleet portal fully tested and working. All data comes from C# API at api.zont.cab. The company profile API shows 0 for numberOfDrivers/vehicleCount but actual drivers/vehicles endpoints return real data (1 each). This is a data inconsistency in the C# backend, not our code. React hydration warnings are from Emergent preview wrapper injecting spans - cosmetic only.

mocked_api.note: All Fleet APIs proxy to real C# backend at https://api.zont.cab - no mocked data
